papers.title: Gemini 1.5: Unlocking multimodal understanding across millions of tokens of context
papers.summary: Mixture-of-experts model with up to 1M-token context, capable of reasoning over hours of video, large code repos, or entire books in a single prompt.

insurance_business_case.headline: Eliminates the chunking gymnastics for long-document insurance use cases: full policy contracts, claim files, and reinsurance treaties go in whole.
points: Group benefits underwriting: ingest a 400-page RFP, census, and prior carrier experience in one shot — produces gap analysis and pricing memo.
Litigation triage: read the full claim file (FNOL → SOAP notes → IME → demand letter) and surface inconsistencies.
Producer training: load the entire product guide + objection handling + compliance disclosure and ask scenario questions.

technical_details.pattern: Long-context LLM as a 'reasoning surface' over case files, with RAG as the precision retrieval layer.
points: Hybrid: RAG narrows to the relevant case file; long-context model reasons across it without further chunking.
Token-cost controls: cap per-case context, cache prior turns with Vertex AI context caching.
PII redaction at ingestion; per-tenant CMEK encryption for jurisdictional data residency.
Eval: needle-in-haystack tests using synthetic clauses planted in real contracts.

papers.title: Med-PaLM 2: Towards Expert-Level Medical Question Answering
papers.summary: Medical-domain LLM that scored expert-level on USMLE-style questions through instruction tuning and ensemble refinement on physician-rated data.

insurance_business_case.headline: Foundation for clinically-grounded medical underwriting and disability claims adjudication.
points: Life underwriting: interprets APS / lab values for impairment classification consistent with manual underwriting guidelines.
Disability/LTC claims: assesses functional capacity from clinician notes, reducing nurse-reviewer queue time.
Group health stop-loss: triages high-cost claimants from medical narratives for early case management.

technical_details.pattern: Domain-adapted LLM with physician-in-the-loop evaluation, deployed behind a clinical-decision-support wrapper.
points: Wrap with structured output schema mapped to underwriting manual codes (e.g., Swiss Re Life Guide).
HITL: every adverse decision routed to a medical director with model rationale + source quotes.
FDA / Health Canada SaMD classification analysis before any 'diagnostic' use.
Bias eval across age/sex/ancestry on historical decisions before deployment.
